message:
refactor(db): overhaul S3-FIFO cache, fix recency cursor pagination, harden sync and reconstruct

s3fifo.rs — Major data structure refactor:
- Replace separate `Location` enum + linear `VecDeque<QueueEntry<K,V>>`
  scans with a unified `Node<V>` enum stored directly in the HashMap.
  The queues (`small`, `main`, `ghost`) now hold only keys (VecDeque<K>),
  while values and frequency counters live in the index HashMap. This
  eliminates O(n) linear scans on every `get()`, `get_mut()`, `insert()`,
  `remove()`, and eviction operation, making them O(1) amortized. The
  previous implementation did a HashMap lookup to find the Location, then
  a full VecDeque scan to find the matching entry — defeating the purpose
  of the cache for large working sets.

cache.rs — Simplify TTL expiration checks:
- Replace verbose three-arm `match` (expired / valid / missing) pattern
  with `get_mut()? + if is_expired()` across all five cache lookup methods
  (get_project, get_project_by_human_key, get_agent_scoped,
  get_agent_by_id_scoped, get_inbox_stats_scoped). The old code had the
  `get_mut()` call happen after the match, but if the entry was None the
  match arm already returned, so the `?` operator achieves the same
  control flow with ~40% less code. The S3-FIFO ghost state is preserved
  because `get_mut()` on a Ghost entry returns None from the S3-FIFO
  perspective (ghosts have no value).

pool.rs — Proactive backup and recovery improvements:
- Flatten nested `if` chain for backup freshness check into `if let`
  chain for readability.
- Add `#[must_use]` to `is_corruption_error_message()`.
- Add comprehensive test suite for `ensure_sqlite_file_healthy_with_archive`:
  backup-preferred recovery, archive-based reconstruction, empty-archive
  reinit fallback, and healthy-DB no-op verification.
- Add proactive backup tests: creation, fresh-skip, memory-DB no-op.

search_planner.rs — Fix recency-mode cursor pagination:
- Recency cursor was broken: all results had `score=0.0`, so cursor
  pagination using `score > ?` would never advance past the first page.
  Now generates a `cursor_score_expr` of `-CAST(COALESCE(m.created_ts,0)
  AS REAL)` for recency mode, encoding the cursor score as negative
  created_ts. This ensures ASC score order maps to newest-first message
  ordering, and cursor-based pagination correctly advances.
- Recency-mode ORDER BY is now `COALESCE(m.created_ts,0) DESC, m.id ASC`
  instead of `score ASC, m.id ASC`, which was meaningless when score=0.
- Add test for recency cursor plan generation.

search_service.rs — Thread ranking mode through cursor computation:
- `compute_next_cursor()` now accepts a `RankingMode` parameter. For
  Recency mode, the cursor score is derived from `-created_ts` (matching
  the planner's expression) instead of the meaningless 0.0 score. This
  fixes multi-page recency search returning duplicate results.
- Add `micros_to_f64_for_cursor()` helper with precision assertion.
- Add tests for recency cursor with/without created_ts.

sync.rs — Fix RETURNING clause and parameterized queries:
- Replace `INSERT ... RETURNING id` with separate `last_insert_rowid()`
  query for the message insert, because not all SQLite builds/wrappers
  support RETURNING. This was causing silent failures on older SQLite
  versions bundled with some Linux distributions.
- Switch all parameterized queries from positional `?` to numbered
  `?1, ?2, ...` style for clarity and to prevent parameter binding
  mismatches when queries are modified.

reconstruct.rs — Robustness improvements:
- Remove unused `project_ids` HashMap (project IDs are already retrieved
  via `query_last_insert_or_existing_id`).
- `discover_messages()` no longer returns `DbResult<()>` since individual
  message parse failures are recorded in stats and should not abort the
  entire reconstruction.
- Support `registered_ts` as a fallback field name for agent inception
  timestamp, since older archives use this key.
- Replace `.and_then(|v| v.as_bool())` with `.and_then(Value::as_bool)`
  and similar clippy-suggested method reference forms.
- Miscellaneous formatting improvements from rustfmt.

schema.rs — Fix migration test invocations:
- Swap `migrate_to_latest` and `migrate_to_latest_base` calls in two
  tests that were calling the wrong variant, causing the tests to either
  skip important migrations or run too many.

queries.rs:
- Fix flaky `since_ts` test by using `saturating_sub(1_000)` to ensure
  the timestamp predicate captures messages created at the boundary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
